automotive-ev-battery.title: Kia EV6 Price Gets Cut to $37,900!
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia has significantly reduced the price of its popular EV6 electric vehicle to $37,900, making it more accessible to mainstream consumers. This price cut strengthens the EV6's competitive position in the affordable electric vehicle market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: $1,523 to Fuel Toyota C-HR vs. $205 to Energize Kia EV6
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A cost comparison analysis demonstrates the significant economic advantage of electric vehicles over traditional combustion engines, with the Kia EV6 requiring just $205 in electricity versus $1,523 in fuel for the Toyota C-HR. This analysis highlights the operational cost savings that drive EV adoption.

automotive-ev-battery.title: An Post halves emissions as EVs top 50% of fleet
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Irish postal operator An Post achieved a 50% reduction in carbon emissions compared to 2009 levels and now operates electric vehicles for more than half of its delivery fleet. The company reached its environmental target three months ahead of schedule.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The 100% Electric VW ID. Polo GTI
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen has introduced the all-electric ID. Polo GTI variant, expanding its electric vehicle portfolio in Europe where it leads in EV sales among traditional automakers. The vehicle demonstrates Volkswagen's commitment to electrification across its entire model lineup.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Chevy Equinox and Blazer EVs gain a few key updates for 2027: Here's what to expect
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chevrolet is implementing several significant updates to its 2027 Equinox EV and Blazer EV models aimed at addressing customer complaints from current model years. These refinements signal General Motors' commitment to improving its electric vehicle offerings.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Charging ahead: Kuala Lumpur, Penang beat EVSE deployment goals
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Malaysian states Kuala Lumpur and Penang have exceeded their annual electric vehicle charger deployment targets ahead of schedule, demonstrating rapid progress in EV infrastructure expansion. These achievements indicate strong regional commitment to accelerating EV adoption in Southeast Asia.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Xiaomi introduces new entry-level and high-end versions of the YU7
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese technology company Xiaomi is expanding its YU7 electric SUV lineup with new entry-level and premium variants targeting different market segments. The new offerings position Xiaomi to compete directly with Tesla and other EV manufacturers across multiple price points.

automotive-ev-battery.title: E-quipment highlight: SANY SY375E packs a SWAPPABLE 550 kWh battery
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese equipment manufacturer SANY has unveiled the SY375E electric excavator featuring an innovative swappable 550 kWh CATL battery system for heavy-duty applications. The swappable battery design represents a significant advancement in making electric equipment viable for intensive job site operations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla now calls FSD 'Tesla Assisted Driving' in China – a more truthful name
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has rebranded its Full Self-Driving system to 'Tesla Assisted Driving' in China, adopting terminology that more accurately reflects the system's current capabilities. The naming change acknowledges regulatory and consumer concerns about overstated autonomous driving claims.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Waymo Robotaxis face challenges on motorways and during flooding
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Autonomous vehicle operator Waymo has revealed significant technical limitations, requiring its robotaxis to avoid highways and suspending operations in four US cities due to performance issues during heavy rainfall and flooding. These setbacks highlight persistent challenges in autonomous vehicle development under adverse conditions.

world-news.title: Trump says Iran deal 'largely negotiated' including reopening Strait of Hormuz
world-news.summary: The Trump administration has announced substantial progress toward a nuclear agreement with Iran, with talks including provisions for reopening the critical Strait of Hormuz shipping channel. Tehran has signaled progress on negotiations but clarified that nuclear weapons remain excluded from the initial framework under discussion.

world-news.title: Rosenberg: Luhansk strike sparks Russian accusations and vow to retaliate
world-news.summary: A strike on the Russian-occupied town of Luhansk in eastern Ukraine killed at least 18 people and wounded 42 others, prompting Moscow to accuse Kyiv of the attack and vow retaliation. The incident underscores escalating military tensions in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict.

world-news.title: Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
world-news.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has vowed retaliatory action after accusing Ukraine of striking a student dormitory, while Ukraine maintains it targeted Russia's elite Rubicon drone military unit in a Moscow-occupied region. The incident reflects the intensifying cycle of military strikes and counterstrikes in the conflict.

world-news.title: Rubio meets Modi during India visit with energy high on agenda
world-news.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is meeting with Indian leadership during a strategic visit focused on energy security, with Washington seeking to increase energy exports to India to offset shortfalls caused by the ongoing Iran conflict. The engagement signals strengthening U.S.-India ties amid geopolitical realignment.

world-news.title: Funerals for medics killed in Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon
world-news.summary: Funeral services were held for paramedics killed in two Israeli air strikes targeting southern Lebanon, continuing a pattern of cross-border military operations. The strikes highlight ongoing tensions between Israel and Lebanese armed groups in the region.

world-news.title: Red Cross volunteers die from suspected Ebola in DR Congo
world-news.summary: Red Cross volunteers in the Democratic Republic of Congo have died from suspected Ebola virus infection, with indications they contracted the disease before the outbreak was formally identified. The deaths underscore challenges in containing the viral threat in the region.

world-news.title: Violence and overcrowding hampers Ebola response in DRC
world-news.summary: The Ebola response in the Democratic Republic of Congo is being significantly impeded by ongoing violence and severe overcrowding in treatment facilities. These barriers are threatening the effectiveness of disease containment and public health interventions.

world-news.title: Tens of thousands rally in Serbia for antigovernment demonstrations
world-news.summary: Tens of thousands of Serbians, primarily students, participated in antigovernment demonstrations calling for early elections, continuing a movement that began following the November 2024 Novi Sad disaster. The protests reflect mounting domestic pressure for political change in the Balkan nation.

world-news.title: At least 82 killed in Chinese coal mine explosion
world-news.summary: An explosion at a coal mine in Shanxi province, China claimed at least 82 lives, marking the nation's deadliest mining disaster in 16 years. The incident raises renewed questions about safety standards and oversight in the Chinese mining industry.

world-news.title: Is pragmatism replacing ideology in international affairs?
world-news.summary: An analysis examines how overlapping interests and competing conflicts are reshaping modern international relations, with pragmatic considerations increasingly supplanting traditional ideological divisions. The shift reflects evolving dynamics in global diplomacy and geopolitical strategy.

finance-markets.title: More War-Driven Inflation Seen in Fed's Favored Gauge
finance-markets.summary: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure is approaching 4% as energy costs spike due to Middle Eastern conflict, raising concerns about broader price pressures across the economy. This development signals potential challenges for monetary policy decisions in the coming months.

finance-markets.title: Bond slump stirs vigilante fears
finance-markets.summary: Rising bond yields have sparked concerns among investment professionals about potential market dysfunction and systemic risks in fixed-income markets. The sharp repricing reflects growing anxiety about inflation and fiscal sustainability.

finance-markets.title: US oil producers increase output to capture price surge from Iran war
finance-markets.summary: American oil companies are ramping up drilling operations to capitalize on surging prices following the Iran conflict, which has driven crude costs up 40 percent. The expansion aims to benefit from global supply constraints, though energy prices remain a political liability for the administration.

finance-markets.title: South-east Asian economies struggle to counter energy shock
finance-markets.summary: Oil-importing nations in Southeast Asia are grappling with energy price inflation and supply disruptions stemming from the Iran conflict, complicating efforts to control broader inflation. Regional policymakers are implementing emergency measures to mitigate economic fallout.

finance-markets.title: Uber Proposes Delivery Hero Takeover at €10 Billion Valuation
finance-markets.summary: Uber Technologies has submitted an offer to acquire German delivery platform Delivery Hero at approximately €10 billion, aiming to strengthen its competitive position against DoorDash in international markets. The proposed deal represents a significant consolidation move in the food delivery sector.

finance-markets.title: World's Most Fervent Day Traders in Korea to Get Risky New Tools
finance-markets.summary: South Korea's volatile stock market will introduce its first single-stock leveraged exchange-traded funds this week, offering retail investors enhanced trading leverage with amplified profit and loss potential. The move raises regulatory concerns about retail investor protection in a market known for high volatility and speculative activity.

finance-markets.title: Trump's 3,711 Trades Point to Multiple Stock-Market Strategies
finance-markets.summary: President Trump's latest financial disclosure reveals an unprecedented scale of trading activity with 3,711 equity transactions across American companies, many with interests subject to federal policy shifts. The disclosure has drawn scrutiny regarding potential conflicts of interest and market transparency.

finance-markets.title: US and Iran move closer to extending ceasefire by 60 days, say mediators
finance-markets.summary: Diplomatic efforts are advancing toward a 60-day ceasefire extension between the US and Iran, with terms including gradual reopening of the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. The agreement would provide relief to global energy markets currently affected by supply disruptions.

finance-markets.title: Hungary's New Premier Warns Predecessor Left Budget 'Skeletons'
finance-markets.summary: Hungary's new Prime Minister Peter Magyar cautioned that fiscal conditions inherited from his predecessor present significant budgetary challenges, limiting the government's ability to make firm economic forecasts. Despite targeting 2% economic growth, Magyar signaled that fiscal constraints may hinder expansionary policies.

finance-markets.title: The new arms race in computing power
finance-markets.summary: Governments falling behind in artificial intelligence capabilities are increasingly viewing data centre technology and experimental computing solutions as critical to military competitiveness. The shift reflects growing recognition that computational power has become integral to modern defence strategy.

tech-ai.title: Google's new anything-to-anything AI model is wild
tech-ai.summary: Google has unveiled a versatile AI model capable of processing and generating multiple types of content, demonstrating significant advances in multimodal artificial intelligence capabilities. The technology raises important questions about the responsible use of generative AI, particularly regarding deepfake creation and potential misuse.

tech-ai.title: Google's AI search is so broken it can 'disregard' what you're looking for
tech-ai.summary: Google's AI Overviews feature is experiencing significant malfunctions, with the system generating nonsensical responses that ignore user search queries. The issues highlight ongoing reliability concerns with Google's AI-powered search integration.

tech-ai.title: These special phone and app features can help protect you from spyware
tech-ai.summary: Apple, Meta, and Google have introduced dedicated security modes designed to protect users from targeted spyware attacks on mobile devices and apps. The article explains how these protective features function and provides guidance on enabling them.

tech-ai.title: Ferrari is using IBM's AI to create F1 superfans
tech-ai.summary: IBM and Scuderia Ferrari are partnering to leverage artificial intelligence technology to enhance and redefine the fan experience in Formula 1 racing. The collaboration demonstrates AI's expanding role in sports entertainment and fan engagement strategies.

tech-ai.title: Peec, one of Berlin's rising startups, more than doubled annualized revenue in months to $10M
tech-ai.summary: Berlin-based startup Peec, which provides AI search presence tracking for brands, has achieved explosive revenue growth by more than doubling its annualized revenue to $10 million in recent months. The company's success highlights a significant emerging trend among European tech startups in the AI monitoring and analytics space.

tech-ai.title: SolarSquare in talks to raise up to $60M as India's rooftop solar market draws major VC interest
tech-ai.summary: Indian rooftop solar company SolarSquare is in advanced financing discussions that could value the company at $500 million and raise up to $60 million. The funding round reflects growing venture capital interest in India's rapidly expanding renewable energy sector.

tech-ai.title: Nuclear startup Deep Fission says it's going public, again, and I have questions
tech-ai.summary: Nuclear technology startup Deep Fission is pursuing an IPO aimed at raising $157 million, though the company's story and valuation face scrutiny from investors. The attempt marks a significant moment in the emerging nuclear innovation sector.

tech-ai.title: Elon Musk has given up on solar power (on Earth)
tech-ai.summary: Elon Musk's companies xAI and SpaceX have shifted their energy strategies away from solar power toward natural gas and orbital data centers respectively, contradicting his previous vision of a solar-electric economy. The strategic pivot raises questions about the feasibility and commitment to renewable energy initiatives.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX's Starship V3 completed a largely successful maiden flight, though significant development work remains before the vehicle is ready for low-Earth orbit missions. The test flight represents progress in SpaceX's ongoing effort to develop a fully reusable super-heavy launch system.

tech-ai.title: Record Club is trying to be Letterboxd for music nerds
tech-ai.summary: A new platform called Record Club aims to fill the gap in the market for a social music discovery and cataloging service similar to Letterboxd for film enthusiasts. The service offers a clean, modern interface designed for music fans to track listening habits and connect with other music lovers more effectively than existing platforms.

science-space.title: Einstein's 'wormhole' may actually reveal a hidden mirror of time
science-space.summary: New research challenges the conventional understanding of wormholes, suggesting Einstein and Rosen's theoretical bridge may reveal bidirectional time flow rather than cosmic tunnels connecting distant spaces. This breakthrough could resolve the long-standing black hole information paradox and propose that the universe existed before the Big Bang.

science-space.title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12: Don't miss these stunning photos from the launch of the most powerful Starship yet
science-space.summary: SpaceX successfully completed its 12th Starship flight test with Starship V3, the most powerful iteration to date, launching from Starbase with notable improvements toward the goal of lunar crewed missions. The flight generated striking launch photography documenting this significant milestone in SpaceX's development program.

science-space.title: Watch China launch Shenzhou 23 astronauts to its Tiangong space station on May 24
science-space.summary: China's Shenzhou 23 mission launched on May 24, delivering a three-person crew to the Tiangong space station to relieve the existing crew and potentially mark the beginning of China's first year-long continuous orbital occupation. The mission represents a significant advancement in China's human spaceflight program.

science-space.title: China reveals 3-person Shenzhou 23 crew, including Hong Kong's 1st astronaut
science-space.summary: China announced the three-astronaut crew for the Shenzhou 23 mission scheduled for May 24, which includes Hong Kong's first astronaut, marking a historic milestone for the region's participation in human spaceflight. This mission demonstrates China's expanding international collaboration in its space program.

science-space.title: Scientists just found a faster, cleaner way to extract lithium for EV batteries
science-space.summary: Researchers at Columbia Engineering have developed a breakthrough lithium-extraction technique using temperature-sensitive solvents to directly pull lithium from underground brines, eliminating the need for massive evaporation ponds that consume years and deplete water supplies. The method also enables extraction from lower-quality lithium sources previously inaccessible to current technologies.

science-space.title: Wegovy linked to rare 'eye stroke' that can cause sudden blindness
science-space.summary: A new analysis of FDA side-effect reports has identified a significantly elevated risk of retinal artery occlusion (eye stroke) associated with Wegovy, the popular weight-loss drug, with the risk signal nearly five times stronger than for Ozempic despite both containing semaglutide. The findings raise important safety concerns for millions of users of the blockbuster medication.

science-space.title: Scientists warn popular vitamin D supplement may have a 'previously unknown' downside
science-space.summary: A surprising study reveals that vitamin D2 supplements may actually reduce the body's levels of vitamin D3, the more biologically active form, while D3 appears to play a unique role in immune response against viruses and bacteria. The findings suggest scientists should reconsider supplementation guidelines to favor D3 over D2.

science-space.title: Scientists warn that current vitamin B12 guidelines may be putting your brain at risk
science-space.summary: UC San Francisco researchers discovered that older adults with vitamin B12 levels considered 'normal' by current guidelines nevertheless exhibited cognitive decline, including slower thinking and delayed visual processing, with visible damage to brain white matter. The study suggests current B12 intake recommendations may be inadequate for protecting aging brain health.

science-space.title: Scientists discover simple way to relieve arthritis pain without pills or surgery
science-space.summary: A year-long clinical trial demonstrated that a subtle adjustment to foot angle during walking reduced knee osteoarthritis pain as effectively as conventional medications while also slowing cartilage damage progression. This low-cost, non-pharmaceutical intervention offers promising relief for millions suffering from knee arthritis.

science-space.title: New AI body map reveals obesity's hidden attack on facial nerves
science-space.summary: Scientists developed an advanced AI-powered system capable of scanning and mapping entire mouse bodies in unprecedented detail, revealing that obesity damages facial sensory nerves responsible for touch and sensation while triggering widespread systemic inflammation. This discovery highlights previously unknown neurological consequences of obesity beyond metabolic disruption.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin tanks to $74,300 as spot ETFs bleed $2.26 billion in two weeks
crypto-web3.summary: U.S.-listed spot bitcoin exchange-traded funds have experienced significant outflows exceeding $2.26 billion over the past two weeks, contributing to downward pressure on bitcoin prices. The sustained capital withdrawal from major bitcoin ETF products signals shifting investor sentiment in the cryptocurrency market.

crypto-web3.title: Spot bitcoin ETFs shed $1.26 billion in worst week since late January as ether funds see 10-day outflow streak
crypto-web3.summary: Spot bitcoin ETFs experienced their worst week of outflows since late January, with BlackRock's IBIT showing significant net asset declines despite large cumulative inflows. Ethereum-focused funds also continued an extended outflow streak, indicating broader weakness in institutional cryptocurrency investment.

crypto-web3.title: SEC Delays Tokenized Stocks Innovation Exemption Amid Concerns: Bloomberg
crypto-web3.summary: The SEC has delayed its expected regulatory framework for tokenized traditional assets, deferring clarity that would have enabled companies to pursue stock tokenization initiatives. The postponement reflects ongoing regulatory concerns about this emerging asset class.

crypto-web3.title: ECB warns EU finance ministers that easing euro stablecoin rules would weaken banks: Reuters
crypto-web3.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde cautioned policymakers that relaxing regulations on euro stablecoins could destabilize bank funding mechanisms and impair interest-rate transmission across the eurozone. The warning underscores regulatory tensions between digital asset innovation and financial stability concerns.

crypto-web3.title: FTX's former law firm, auditor agree to pay $66 million to settle customer claims over fraud
crypto-web3.summary: Law firm Fenwick & West and FTX's former auditor have agreed to a $66 million settlement to resolve customer claims related to FTX's fraud, though the firm continues to face separate litigation including a $525 million lawsuit. The settlement represents partial accountability for professional service providers implicated in the exchange's collapse.

crypto-web3.title: Clarity Act could spark a boom in crypto 'yield-as-a-service'
crypto-web3.summary: Proposed legislation may redirect the cryptocurrency yield market away from passive holding models toward compliance-focused, AI-driven yield infrastructure platforms. The regulatory shift could create new business opportunities for yield-as-a-service providers in the digital asset space.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin is ready to beat stocks and bonds again after underperformance against Wall Street
crypto-web3.summary: Prominent portfolio strategist Mark Connors argues that bitcoin has exited its longest period of underperformance relative to traditional assets and is positioned to outperform stocks, bonds, and gold as inflation remains elevated. The assessment reflects renewed optimism about bitcoin's long-term value proposition amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

crypto-web3.title: NEAR token jumps 30% amid AI hype, Arthur Hayes endorsement and scaling plans
crypto-web3.summary: NEAR Protocol's native token surged 30% following endorsement from prominent crypto investor Arthur Hayes, who identified it as part of an altcoin cluster alongside HYPE and ZEC. The price appreciation reflects renewed market interest in layer-one blockchain platforms with AI capabilities and scaling solutions.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto trader sees Hyperliquid, AI tokens leading next altcoin rally
crypto-web3.summary: Technical analyst Michael van de Poppe identifies Hyperliquid's recent performance surge and renewed institutional interest in AI-focused cryptocurrency projects as indicators of broader altcoin market recovery. The observations suggest a returning appetite for higher-risk digital asset categories.

crypto-web3.title: Kalshi-backed prediction market advocacy group debuts with support from former Trump aide
crypto-web3.summary: A new advocacy organization focused on prediction market regulation has launched with backing from former White House Deputy Chief of Staff Taylor Budowich, signaling increased political engagement around digital prediction platforms. The initiative reflects broader efforts to secure regulatory clarity for emerging financial infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: Digital Banking Startup Mercury Lands $200M At $5.2B Valuation Amid Fintech Funding Uptick
startups-vc.summary: Mercury has raised $200 million in a Series D funding round at a $5.2 billion valuation, representing a 49% increase from its previous $3.5 billion valuation achieved in March 2025. The raise reflects strong momentum in fintech funding and validates digital banking's market opportunity.

startups-vc.title: Embodied AI Fuels Record Robotics Funding In China As IPO Momentum Builds
startups-vc.summary: China-based robotics companies have raised $5.6 billion across 176 deals through mid-May 2026, matching the entire year's total from 2021 and signaling record venture funding momentum. The surge reflects strong market confidence in embodied AI and robotics innovation.

startups-vc.title: SolarSquare in talks to raise up to $60M as India's rooftop solar market draws major VC interest
startups-vc.summary: SolarSquare is in advanced discussions to raise up to $60 million in funding, with an expected valuation of up to $500 million upon close next month. The financing highlights growing venture capital interest in India's rooftop solar energy sector.

startups-vc.title: BillionToOne Goes Public — The Startup That Made Genetic Testing Universal
startups-vc.summary: BillionToOne, a Y Combinator-backed biotech company, has gone public, becoming the fourth YC biotech company to reach IPO status. The company has established significant market presence with its fetal genetic test now used for approximately 1 in 11 babies born in the United States.

startups-vc.title: Meesho Goes Public
startups-vc.summary: Meesho has completed its initial public offering, marking the graduation of a Y Combinator S16 cohort company to public markets. The platform has established itself as one of India's largest e-commerce businesses, empowering millions of small merchants.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to Groww
startups-vc.summary: Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, has gone public, marking Y Combinator's first company to reach IPO status in India. The platform serves tens of millions of users providing access to stocks, mutual funds, and ETFs.

startups-vc.title: Peec, one of Berlin's rising startups, more than doubled annualized revenue in months to $10M, sources say
startups-vc.summary: Peec, a Berlin-based startup that helps brands track their presence in AI search results, has more than doubled its annualized revenue to $10 million in recent months. The growth demonstrates a key trend among European startups capitalizing on AI-driven market opportunities.

startups-vc.title: After A 6-Figure Fertility Journey, This Founder Built An IVF Startup With 'Outcome Protection'
startups-vc.summary: Gaia is an AI-powered fertility startup that leverages machine learning trained on millions of anonymized historical fertility outcomes to assess risk and probability for IVF treatment. The startup addresses a significant gap in fertility care by using data science to improve patient outcomes.

startups-vc.title: Clouted wants to take the guesswork out of making short videos go viral
startups-vc.summary: Clouted, a video clipping startup focused on making short-form video creation more predictable, has secured $7 million in seed funding led by Slow Ventures. The funding reflects investor confidence in tools that systematize viral video creation.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has appointed Harshita Arora as a General Partner, bringing fintech and infrastructure expertise alongside founder experience spanning her teenage years through multiple ventures. The addition strengthens YC's investment capability in financial services and infrastructure sectors.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A significant data extortion attack targeting the Canvas education technology platform has disrupted classes nationwide, with cybercriminals defacing the login page and threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions. The breach represents a critical threat to the education sector affecting millions of users across the United States.

cybersecurity.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
cybersecurity.summary: GitHub has confirmed a data breach involving the theft of thousands of internal developer code repositories, with threat actor TeamPCP claiming responsibility. The breach raises significant concerns about the security of critical software development infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Laravel-Lang PHP Packages Compromised to Deliver Cross-Platform Credential Stealer
cybersecurity.summary: Multiple PHP packages in the Laravel-Lang ecosystem have been compromised in a supply chain attack designed to deliver a credential-stealing framework to developers. The attack targeted popular packages including laravel-lang/lang, laravel-lang/http-statuses, laravel-lang/attributes, and laravel-lang/actions.

cybersecurity.title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Bug Actively Exploited, Added to CISA KEV
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has added a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Drupal Core to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog following evidence of active exploitation in the wild. The flaw, CVE-2026-9082, affects all supported versions of Drupal Core and poses an immediate risk to thousands of websites.

cybersecurity.title: LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin CVE-2026-48172 Exploited to Run Scripts as Root
cybersecurity.summary: A maximum-severity privilege escalation vulnerability in the LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin has come under active exploitation, allowing attackers to run arbitrary scripts with elevated root permissions. The flaw, rated 10.0 on the CVSS scale, poses a critical risk to cPanel users and hosting providers.

cybersecurity.title: Germany Doxes "UNKN," Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
cybersecurity.summary: German authorities have identified and exposed the operator behind the notorious REvil and GandCrab ransomware gangs as 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin, who orchestrated at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion between 2019 and 2021. The identification represents a significant law enforcement victory against organized cybercrime.

cybersecurity.title: Claude Mythos AI Finds 10,000 High-Severity Flaws in Widely Used Software
cybersecurity.summary: Anthropic's Project Glasswing initiative has uncovered over 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities in systemically important software worldwide since launching last month, demonstrating the effectiveness of AI-powered security research. The findings highlight both the capabilities of AI in identifying security defects and the widespread vulnerability landscape in critical infrastructure software.

cybersecurity.title: Packagist Supply Chain Attack Infects 8 Packages Using GitHub-Hosted Linux Malware
cybersecurity.summary: A coordinated supply chain attack has compromised eight packages on the Packagist repository with malicious code targeting projects shipping JavaScript, with the malware designed to retrieve and execute a Linux binary from GitHub. The attack demonstrates evolving tactics in software supply chain threats affecting the PHP and JavaScript development communities.

cybersecurity.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: GitHub has launched staged publishing controls for npm, now generally available, requiring maintainers to pass two-factor authentication challenges before packages become publicly available for installation. The security enhancement represents a significant step forward in protecting the software supply chain against unauthorized package distribution.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle are releasing near-record volumes of security patches this month, reflecting AI systems' growing capability in identifying vulnerabilities in human-written code. The surge in bug fixes underscores the dual-edged nature of AI in cybersecurity, where advanced tools are simultaneously improving threat detection and remediation.

health-biotech.title: Lilly's triple-acting obesity drug hits goal in Phase 3 trial
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly's experimental triple-acting obesity drug achieved its Phase 3 trial endpoint, with participants losing approximately one-quarter of their body weight. The result potentially establishes a new efficacy benchmark for obesity treatments and intensifies competition with established players like Novo Nordisk in this high-value therapeutic market.

health-biotech.title: Parkinson's drug from Biogen, Denali comes up short
health-biotech.summary: Biogen and Denali terminated their mid-stage trial of an experimental Parkinson's therapy after it failed to slow disease progression in a randomized clinical trial. The companies have decided to discontinue the combined program, though Denali intends to pursue independent development focused on specific patient subpopulations.

health-biotech.title: EU and US advisers split over AstraZeneca breast cancer drug
health-biotech.summary: Regulatory authorities in the European Union and United States have diverged in their assessment of an AstraZeneca breast cancer therapy, with a Merck-KeLun antibody-drug conjugate demonstrating superior performance against Keytruda in clinical trials. The regulatory disagreement underscores ongoing differences in approval standards between major markets.

health-biotech.title: ASCO26: 5 data snapshots ahead of the year's biggest cancer drug meeting
health-biotech.summary: Clinical trial abstracts released ahead of the American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting provide early insights into major oncology datasets from leading companies including Merck, BioNTech, Eli Lilly, and Moderna. The data presentations will shape industry discussion around emerging cancer treatment options across multiple tumor types.

health-biotech.title: In Alzheimer's, Bristol Myers sees big promise beyond amyloid
health-biotech.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb's neuroscience leadership team is pursuing Alzheimer's disease therapies targeting mechanisms beyond amyloid pathology as the company seeks to establish its presence in neurology. The strategic shift reflects evolving understanding of Alzheimer's biology and positions Bristol Myers to diversify its neuroscience portfolio.

health-biotech.title: BioMarin notches win in study that could expand use of top-selling medicine
health-biotech.summary: BioMarin Pharmaceutical achieved positive Phase 3 results that could expand the clinical applications of Voxzogo, strengthening its market position amid growing competitive pressures. The success follows a separate Phase 3 setback for another investigational drug and reinforces BioMarin's growth trajectory.

health-biotech.title: After raising $800M, Parabilis seeks an IPO to pursue 'undruggable' targets
health-biotech.summary: Parabilis Biotech is pursuing an initial public offering following substantial private financing to advance its pipeline targeting challenging biological targets previously considered undruggable. The IPO represents part of a robust biotech funding environment, with 2026 seeing average biotech IPOs exceeding $286 million.

health-biotech.title: Congress slashed Medicaid funding to providers. The Trump administration wants to cut even further
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration has announced plans to reduce state-directed payments to Medicaid providers beyond reductions already implemented by Congress, setting the stage for conflict with healthcare provider organizations. The proposed cuts could significantly impact provider reimbursement and healthcare delivery systems nationwide.

health-biotech.title: Longevity startup Retro Biosciences says latest fundraising values it at $1.8 billion
health-biotech.summary: Retro Biosciences, a longevity-focused biotechnology startup backed by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, has secured additional funding at a $1.8 billion valuation. The funding round reflects growing investor interest in interventions targeting human aging and lifespan extension.

health-biotech.title: Tender offer for Recordati; Sobi's gout drug passes Phase 3 test
health-biotech.summary: Investment firms CVC Capital Partners and Groupe Bruxelles Lambert have launched a cash tender offer for pharmaceutical company Recordati, while Swedish Orphan Biopharma achieved Phase 3 success with an experimental gout treatment. The developments highlight ongoing consolidation activity and advancement of therapeutics in rare disease segments.

energy-climate.title: New coal plants hit '10-year' global high in 2025 – but power output still fell
energy-climate.summary: Global coal-fired power plant construction reached a decade-high in 2025, though overall coal power generation continued to decline amid renewable energy expansion. The trend reflects divergent regional strategies, with some nations still investing in coal infrastructure while others accelerate clean energy transitions.

energy-climate.title: US summer generating capacity increases by 75 GW since 2025: FERC
energy-climate.summary: The U.S. added 75 GW of summer generating capacity since 2025, with significant contributions from Texas, the Western Electric Coordinating Council region, and the Midcontinent Independent System Operator market. This substantial capacity expansion reflects ongoing grid modernization and renewable energy deployment across multiple interconnects.

energy-climate.title: EU underestimating rooftop PV power generation
energy-climate.summary: SolarPower Europe reports that official EU solar generation statistics significantly undercount actual photovoltaic output, with real 2025 generation estimated at 410 TWh versus 275 TWh in official figures. The discrepancy stems from incomplete rooftop PV registration, data delays, and difficulty measuring self-consumed solar electricity.

energy-climate.title: Mandatory utility fees distort price signals for rooftop solar in the US
energy-climate.summary: Twenty-seven U.S. states are implementing rate designs that shift residential electricity pricing toward higher fixed monthly charges and lower usage-based rates, undermining the economic case for rooftop solar and battery systems. These structural changes weaken incentives for distributed renewable energy and may increase long-term electricity costs by reinforcing reliance on centralized grid expansion.

energy-climate.title: Enbridge, Meta to build 365-MW solar/200-MW storage project
energy-climate.summary: Enbridge and Meta are partnering to develop a 365-MW solar facility with 200-MW battery storage in Wyoming at an anticipated cost of $1.2 billion, with commercial operation expected by end of 2027. The project demonstrates growing corporate investment in large-scale renewable energy with storage integration.

energy-climate.title: UK grants development consent for offshore wind farms
energy-climate.summary: The UK government approved development consent for the Dogger Bank South and North Falls offshore wind farm projects, advancing major renewable energy infrastructure. These approvals represent significant progress in the nation's offshore wind expansion strategy.

energy-climate.title: Brazil falls short of its battery storage potential
energy-climate.summary: Brazil's energy storage industry leaders are addressing systemic challenges in the electricity sector by advocating for integrated solutions combining battery storage, thermal power, and renewables. The discussion emphasizes balancing security, cost, renewable generation, and grid flexibility rather than viewing competing technologies as alternatives.

energy-climate.title: Nuclear fuel is the weak link in US energy security: Centrus CMO
energy-climate.summary: Centrus Energy's Chief Marketing Officer warns that the U.S. nuclear fuel supply chain presents a critical vulnerability in national energy security amid geopolitical competition. He advocates for industry-wide collaboration to rebuild domestic capacity and reduce dependence on external suppliers.

energy-climate.title: Europe creates map to locate available capacity in power grids
energy-climate.summary: A new pan-European digital platform has been established to enhance transparency regarding available hosting capacity in European electricity grids across transmission and distribution networks. The tool aims to improve grid access information and facilitate renewable energy integration.

energy-climate.title: AEP, other Ohio utilities could own nuclear power plants under state bill
energy-climate.summary: A proposed Ohio bill would permit utilities including AEP to directly own and operate nuclear power plants, though the Ohio Manufacturers' Association opposes the measure citing customer risk exposure. The legislation reflects ongoing debate over utility business model reform and nuclear energy policy.

asia-pacific.title: Rubio renews US ties with India after Trump's China visit
asia-pacific.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio visited India and met Prime Minister Narendra Modi, inviting him to Washington and reaffirming India as a natural US partner. The visit comes one week after Trump's state visit to Beijing, signaling Washington's efforts to balance relations with both major Asian powers amid evolving geopolitical dynamics.

asia-pacific.title: Why Bangladesh could raise Indian red flags by buying a China-Pakistan fighter jet
asia-pacific.summary: Bangladesh has received a JF-17 Thunder Block III combat aircraft simulator from Pakistan, a development that analysts warn could escalate tensions with India amid already strained Dhaka-New Delhi relations. The acquisition represents a significant military modernization by Bangladesh and highlights shifting defense partnerships in South Asia.

asia-pacific.title: South Korea's Nuclear Submarine Push Is a Test of Non-nuclear Deterrence
asia-pacific.summary: South Korea is advancing its nuclear submarine capabilities as part of a broader effort to strengthen deterrence against North Korea while maintaining its non-nuclear weapons status. The initiative tests the viability of conventional submarine technology as an alternative to nuclear armament in the region.

asia-pacific.title: Japan's Frigate Diplomacy — and What It Could Mean for Taiwan
asia-pacific.summary: Japan is expanding maritime security cooperation with regional partners, potentially positioning itself as a second major democratic defense partner for Taiwan's military modernization. This diplomatic initiative reflects Tokyo's growing commitment to supporting Taiwan's defense capabilities amid regional security concerns.

asia-pacific.title: Budget cuts leave Thailand's twin-sea navy at risk of falling behind
asia-pacific.summary: Thailand's Royal Thai Navy faces budget constraints that threaten its ability to effectively monitor and defend both the Gulf of Thailand and the Andaman Sea, critical maritime zones for regional trade and strategic interests. The funding shortfalls complicate the navy's strategic objectives outlined in its 2023 white paper.

asia-pacific.title: Pyongyang Recalibrates: North Korea Between Russia, the United States, and Japan
asia-pacific.summary: North Korea is repositioning itself diplomatically among Russia, the United States, and Japan, moving beyond its recent alignment with Moscow. The recalibration reflects evolving strategic calculations regarding regional security and diplomatic engagement.

asia-pacific.title: Japan Doubles Down on Maritime Security Cooperation
asia-pacific.summary: Japan is intensifying its maritime security partnerships with regional allies, demonstrating Tokyo's commitment to strengthening defense cooperation in Asia-Pacific waters. The initiative underscores Japan's strategic pivot toward enhanced naval presence and collaborative security measures.

asia-pacific.title: 'Let's give it a try': motion sickness no barrier for Hong Kong's first astronaut
asia-pacific.summary: Lai Ka-ying, Hong Kong's first astronaut, completed nearly two years of training to join China's Shenzhou-23 mission to the Tiangong space station, launching on Sunday night. Her selection represents a milestone for Hong Kong's participation in China's space exploration program despite physical limitations.

asia-pacific.title: Japan and South Korea: Friends, With Limits
asia-pacific.summary: Japan and South Korea are strengthening diplomatic ties, though North Korea remains a fundamental point of division between the two neighbors. The relationship reflects cautious cooperation balanced against persistent regional security concerns.

asia-pacific.title: The US-China Summit from a Japanese Perspective
asia-pacific.summary: Japan views recent US-China diplomatic engagement with cautious concern, anticipating continued geopolitical tensions despite rhetorical efforts at engagement. The summit's outcomes carry significant implications for Japan's regional security strategy and its own US alliance dynamics.
